title: Prisma schema changed but `prisma generate` not run — types are stale
summary: Agent modifies schema.prisma but forgets to run `npx prisma generate`, causing TypeScript errors or runtime crashes from stale client types.
mistake: After editing `schema.prisma` (adding a model, changing a field), the agent runs `npx prisma db push` or `npx prisma migrate dev` to sync the DB, but forgets to run `npx prisma generate` to regenerate the TypeScript client. The code then references new fields that the Prisma Client doesn't know about, causing either TypeScript compilation errors or runtime `Unknown field` errors. Agent enters a retry loop trying to fix 'type errors' by editing the schema again.
instead: After ANY change to `schema.prisma`, ALWAYS run `npx prisma generate` immediately after the migration/push command. Better yet, use `npx prisma migrate dev` which runs generate automatically. If using `db push`, chain: `npx prisma db push && npx prisma generate`. Add a `postinstall` script in package.json: `"postinstall": "prisma generate"` for CI environments.
